constraints_1_content: Before writing solution.sh, use list_dir to explore /app/projects to understand the actual directory structure, nesting depth, and distribution of dependency file types present in the test environment.
constraints_1_rationale: This is specific to the /app/projects layout of this task and uses the SWETraeAgent list_dir tool.
constraints_3_content: After writing solution.sh, run `bash -n /app/solution.sh` via run_command to check for syntax errors before executing the script functionally.
constraints_3_rationale: Bash syntax pre-check is specific to this shell-script task; uses the run_command tool.
constraints_4_content: After the full implementation, validate output with run_command: `APP_ROOT=/app bash /app/solution.sh && head -1 /app/out/conflicts.csv && head -1 /app/out/dependency_graph.csv` to confirm both files are created and carry the correct headers.
constraints_4_rationale: Verifying both CSV headers is specific to this task's output format; uses run_command.
constraints_5_content: Use search_by_regex to scan solution.sh for hardcoded string `/app` (outside of variable assignments) and replace any occurrences with the appropriate `$APP_ROOT`, `$PROJECTS`, or `$OUT` variables.
constraints_5_rationale: The portability concern about hardcoded paths is specific to this task's variable-driven path design; uses search_by_regex.
constraints_6_content: When embedding Python code inside Bash heredocs, always use single-quoted delimiters (e.g., `<<'PYEOF'`) to prevent unintended shell variable expansion inside the Python source.
constraints_6_rationale: Relevant specifically to Python-in-Bash heredoc patterns used in this task; does not reference any framework tool.
constraints_7_content: For TOML parsing, prefer `python3 -c "import tomllib; ..."` (Python 3.11+); if tomllib is unavailable, fall back to `tomli` import — do NOT attempt to install packages via pip at runtime.
constraints_7_rationale: Specific to the Cargo.toml parsing requirement of this task; no framework tool involved.
